title: equisetum ramosissimum subsp. campanulatum
intro: Equisetum ramosissimum subsp. campanulatum, also known as Japanese horsetail, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the family Equisetaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being rich in silica, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Japanese horsetail include its potential to support kidney function, reduce inflammation, and improve skin health, as well as its antioxidant and diuretic properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
